steps.title: Identify the modifying phrase
steps.content: The opening “Home to roughly 710,000 people,” is a participial phrase that introduces the sentence. This phrase must logically modify the subject of the main clause — and that subject must be something that can actually be “home to” people (a location, not a statistic).

steps.title: Match the subject to the phrase
steps.content:
Choice A places “the city of Riga” as the subject, so the city (a location) logically can be home to 710,000 people.

Choice B lacks a proper main verb and reads as a sentence fragment.

Choices C and D both attempt to make “an impressive 35 percent” the subject — but a percentage cannot logically be “home to” people, creating a dangling modifier.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
